..... 19 4 Confidential Document - HR  COMPANY OVERVIEW  Code of Conduct Ethical behavior is an essential part of your position with LOS and is a personal responsibility that should be taken very seriously. We need and expect each employee to be accountable for their work and behavior and to support the values, principles, and standards upon which LOS's business reputation rests. This Code of Conduct (the "Code") is a guide to help you live up to LOS’s values, abide by LOS's policies and respe

nduct (the "Code") is a guide to help you live up to LOS’s values, abide by LOS's policies and respect the best interests of our customers and shareholders. The purpose of the Code is to describe principles of business conduct and to provide guidance in ethical decision-making. Many sections of the Code reference matters for which specific policies exist; this is because the Code encompasses standards of behavior outlined in other LOS policies. You are expected to become familiar with LOS polici

ards of behavior outlined in other LOS policies. You are expected to become familiar with LOS policies that directly impact your daily work. Although questionable activities may not be mentioned in this Code, ask yourself the following questions to decide if an activity should be reported or discussed with your manager: • Does it seem right? • Is it legal? • Does it match up with LOS’s vision and values? • Would I feel uncomfortable if I read about it in a newspaper? • Would other people at LOS

values? • Would I feel uncomfortable if I read about it in a newspaper? • Would other people at LOS feel uncomfortable if they read about it in a Newspaper? Although the Code lays out fundamental principles of ethical and legal conduct, it cannot anticipate every ethical dilemma or situation you may encounter in performing your daily work. If at any time you are unsure about the proper course of action discuss your concerns with your manager. Application of Code The Code applies to all employees

ction discuss your concerns with your manager. Application of Code The Code applies to all employees, contractors, and the members of the Board of Directors, which for purposes of the Code are referred to as "Employees". Contractors include consultants, suppliers, and vendors who must have access to LOS’s confidential, business, and proprietary information to perform their duties. 5 Confidential Document - HR Compliance Compliance with the Code is mandatory. You are expected to be familiar with

ocument - HR Compliance Compliance with the Code is mandatory. You are expected to be familiar with and comply with the Code in the performance of your duties. As well, you are expected to understand your obligations under LOS policies. Those of us who fail to abide by the Code and LOS's policies will be subject to disciplinary action, up to and including dismissal or prosecution. Responsibilities to report It is the responsibility of everybody at LOS to report any known or suspected unethical c

to report It is the responsibility of everybody at LOS to report any known or suspected unethical conduct, which includes any violation of the Code, by other Employees or anyone in any way associated with the company. LOS is committed to protecting all Employees who report unethical conduct from reprisal as well as offering any necessary support to individuals who make reports. When an Employee makes a report of unethical conduct they should do so in good faith. Detailed in the Whistleblower se

akes a report of unethical conduct they should do so in good faith. Detailed in the Whistleblower section of the Code are the reporting procedures for Employees who suspect or become aware of any unethical conduct.  Our Business Conduct The Non-Disclosure Agreement/Policy confirms in writing the Non-Disclosure Policies and practices that LOS follows. The NDA extends to all Employees of LOS, its board of directors, those authorized to speak on its behalf, and other people with access to undisclo

oard of directors, those authorized to speak on its behalf, and other people with access to undisclosed material information. It covers: Disclosure and documents filed with securities regulators; Financial and non-financial disclosure, including Management's Discussion and Analysis Written statements made in LOS's annual and quarterly reports, news releases, and letters to shareholders, and presentations by senior management and other Employees; Electronic communications through e-mail, social n

tations by senior management and other Employees; Electronic communications through e-mail, social networking sites, and LOS's website, including audio and video content; and Oral statements made in meetings and telephone conversations with analysts and investors, interviews with media, as well as speeches, press conferences, and conference calls. 6 Confidential Document - HR Conduct when representing LOS Conduct yourself professionally and with personal integrity, both in and out of the workpla

ting LOS Conduct yourself professionally and with personal integrity, both in and out of the workplace, reflective of LOS values. Communicate and negotiate with honesty with all Employees, customers, partners, stakeholders, suppliers, associates, and other members of the public. Our obligation to act with integrity and within the spirit of this Code continues while traveling, whether domestically or abroad. Legal and social responsibility Ensure that your actions comply with and are within the m

or abroad. Legal and social responsibility Ensure that your actions comply with and are within the meaning and intent of all applicable laws and regulations. Ensure that your actions are free from suspicion and criticism and have no unfavorable effects on society being associated with LOS. Conduct with customers Serving customers is the focal point of our business. Our customers deserve the highest quality service and standards in all transactions. Provide our customers with value and deal with

t quality service and standards in all transactions. Provide our customers with value and deal with them fairly. Act with integrity and do everything possible to provide great service to our customers, either directly or by supporting the work of other individuals or units. Do not make promises that you or LOS cannot keep. Use of Company’s Private Information Do not disclose information about LOS activities or our customers’ activities to non-authorized Employees within the workplace, or anyone

activities or our customers’ activities to non-authorized Employees within the workplace, or anyone outside the workplace unless following LOS's NDA. All information held by LOS is confidential and the property of LOS. This includes information relating to LOS business, property, Employees, customers, partners, consultants, or others that are not generally available to the public. In our business, we become aware of confidential information about our customer's business and personal ventures. We

we become aware of confidential information about our customer's business and personal ventures. We have a responsibility to uphold the trust of our customers. Do not discuss or disclose confidential customer information outside the workplace and ensure that uses of 7 Confidential Document - HR confidential customer information within LOS are consistent with the purposes for which it was collected. Our obligation to keep corporate information confidential continues post-employment as well. Use

d. Our obligation to keep corporate information confidential continues post-employment as well. Use of computer systems and software Our computer systems and software form the backbone of our services and our operations infrastructure. Every effort should be made to protect LOS's computer systems and associated software from various threats to their security such as accidental or deliberate destruction of data or equipment, interruption of service, disclosure of confidential information, and the

ction of data or equipment, interruption of service, disclosure of confidential information, and theft or corruption of data. Any security concerns concerning our systems or software, or any viruses or data network attacks, weaknesses, or unexplained system changes should immediately be reported to the Manager of Facilities, Risk, and Security. Use of company property Protect LOS’s physical property and revenues. Company property includes, but is not limited to: premises, equipment, supplies, fu

ty and revenues. Company property includes, but is not limited to: premises, equipment, supplies, furnishings, Employee search accounts, funds, reports, records, vehicles, trade secrets, computer software, hardware and networks, internet accounts, and intangible items such as the details of business application systems. Ensure that company property under our control is used following LOS’s guidelines only. Employees are responsible for ensuring that:  LOS's assets are protected and not used for

nes only. Employees are responsible for ensuring that:  LOS's assets are protected and not used for personal use unless otherwise authorized;  LOS's intellectual property is subject to copyright and is not reproduced, distributed, or altered without authorization;  as far as practicable, contracts are put in writing; and  unless otherwise authorized LOS's name or purchasing power is not used to obtain personal benefits, discounts, or rebates. Proper Reporting of Financial Transactions You ar

o obtain personal benefits, discounts, or rebates. Proper Reporting of Financial Transactions You are accountable for any corporate funds over which you have control. Follow the set procedures for handling, recording, and protecting funds. When spending LOS funds, ensure that LOS receives good value for the expenditure. Do not use LOS funds for personal benefit. 8 Confidential Document - HR We must:  Maintain accurate and reliable records relating to business, customers, and employees to meet o

t:  Maintain accurate and reliable records relating to business, customers, and employees to meet our legal and financial obligations and follow our accounting and business practices;  Not intentionally cause LOS's books or records to be incorrect or misleading in any way and relevant information should never be omitted, deleted, or concealed;  Not create or participate in the creation of any record intended to conceal anything improper;  Properly and promptly record all disbursements of fun

ecord intended to conceal anything improper;  Properly and promptly record all disbursements of funds;  Cooperate and ensure full disclosure in communications with both internal and external auditors and with the concerned authority;  Promptly disclose knowledge of any untruthful or inaccurate statements or records whether intentionally or unintentionally made;  Promptly bring to the attention of your manager any transactions that did not seem to have a legitimate commercial purpose; and  I

of your manager any transactions that did not seem to have a legitimate commercial purpose; and  Information must only be destroyed following approved retention schedules and procedures.  Our Work environment Employee Professionalism We are all committed to supporting a safe, healthy, and positive workplace for all Employees. We will not tolerate behavior that interferes with an Employee’s ability to perform his or her duties. The use or effects of alcohol or illegal drugs are not acceptable

ity to perform his or her duties. The use or effects of alcohol or illegal drugs are not acceptable in our work environment. Equity Respect the rights, culture, and dignity of all individuals and adhere to the principles of equity and non-discrimination when dealing with Employees, customers, suppliers, and others. We will not tolerate any form of discrimination or harassment, following applicable human rights legislation. 9 Confidential Document - HR Employee privacy We are all committed to pro

human rights legislation. 9 Confidential Document - HR Employee privacy We are all committed to protecting the privacy of Employees' personal information. Employee personal information will not be collected, used, or disclosed other than as authorized or as required for business reasons. Conflict of Interest We must not engage in any activities which could give rise to, or could be perceived to give rise to, a conflict of interest. As Employees, managers, executives, and members of the Board of

ive rise to, a conflict of interest. As Employees, managers, executives, and members of the Board of Directors, our business loyalty rests in placing LOS's interests, including those of its customers and shareholders, before our interests. A “conflict of interest” arises in a situation where your activities,  Interests or dealings may, potentially, or be perceived to:  Impair your ability to perform your duties as a LOS Employee;  Harm to LOS’s reputation; or  Result in a personal gain or ad

form your duties as a LOS Employee;  Harm to LOS’s reputation; or  Result in a personal gain or advantage due to your position in LOS We must not use our position to influence or bypass LOS procedures for personal gain or the personal gain of a family member, friend, colleague, or anyone else. Conflicts of interest negatively impact both you and LOS. Whether or not an actual or potential conflict of interest exists depends upon the facts of each case. If you become aware that a conflict of int

t of interest exists depends upon the facts of each case. If you become aware that a conflict of interest might exist, seek clarification and guidance before proceeding or, if the activity has commenced, immediately cease the activity and disclose it in writing to the concerned authority as soon as possible. The examples cited in this Code are not an exhaustive list of all potential conflicts of interest. Use your discretion and be sure to feel comfortable in every situation you encounter. Remem

nterest. Use your discretion and be sure to feel comfortable in every situation you encounter. Remember that when in doubt as to whether or not you are in a conflict of interest situation, disclosure is the best policy. If you have any questions about conflicts of interest, please contact HR for a detailed discussion. 10 Confidential Document - HR Involvement in political activity You may participate in the political process at any level of government providing that your involvement does not int

ate in the political process at any level of government providing that your involvement does not interfere with your work or the effectiveness of your position. Ensure that any political activity does not place you in a conflict of interest situation with LOS. Gift Acceptance Gifts or benefits of any kind must not be given or received by an Employee or their immediate family when it might be perceived that an obligation is created or favor is expected. The giving and receiving of gifts and promo

ived that an obligation is created or favor is expected. The giving and receiving of gifts and promotional items of modest value are acceptable as is reasonable entertainment, if within the limits of responsible and generally accepted business practices. Never solicit or accept a personal benefit as a condition of performing your duties. Payments to Agents, Consultants, Government Officials, and Others Payments of any nature, which would violate any law, are prohibited. All payments of commissio

thers Payments of any nature, which would violate any law, are prohibited. All payments of commissions and fees shall be following sound business practices. Payments, gifts, or favors must not be made to any person with the intent to induce them to violate their duties or to obtain favorable treatment for the Employee or LOS.  Whistleblower Reporting LOS acknowledges and understands that whistleblowing is an activity that is stressful and which, without appropriate protections, can be a risky u

lowing is an activity that is stressful and which, without appropriate protections, can be a risky undertaking for an Employee. This section intends to:  provide a mechanism for reporting actual or potential unethical conduct to a designated authority for investigation and appropriate action;  protect those Employees who report unethical conduct following this section from undue negative repercussions; and  protect those who may be wrongly or falsely accused through effective investigation pr

ssions; and  protect those who may be wrongly or falsely accused through effective investigation procedures. 11 Confidential Document - HR Whistleblowing is the reporting by Employees, contractors, or Board Members of incidents of unethical conduct that are under the control of their employer, to persons or organizations that may be able to take action to address the unethical conduct. At LOS, whistleblowing includes any incident of unethical conduct or violation of the Code. Unethical Conduct

istleblowing includes any incident of unethical conduct or violation of the Code. Unethical Conduct includes any serious act or omission intentional or not which is contrary to LOS’s policies, operating procedures, or the Code, that is illegal, unethical, and immoral with serious or negative implications for the public interest and the integrity of the organization. 12 Confidential Document - HR  Policies  1. Meal and break policy  Breaks (Lunch/Rest) are not included in office hours.  Emplo

Policies  1. Meal and break policy  Breaks (Lunch/Rest) are not included in office hours.  Employees can go for lunch break between 1:15 PM to 2:45 PM.  Rest breaks must not be longer than 15 minutes and not more than twice a day.  Eatery items are not allowed to eat during working hours.  Emergency Breaks: Emergencies are not neither planned nor certain, we are humans and emergencies are need to be taken care of.  In case of emergency, simply talk to your immediate reporter asking for r

eed to be taken care of.  In case of emergency, simply talk to your immediate reporter asking for required break so that you emergency can be accommodated.  2. Leave Policy i. Planned Paid leave-  Leave year is counted as calendar year, it starts from January 1 and ends on December 31.  Every leave, other than emergencies, must be a planned leave.  Confirmed employees are entitled for 8 days of ‘Planned Leaves’ (on pro-rata basis).  Leaves will automatically be credited in your leave regis

of ‘Planned Leaves’ (on pro-rata basis).  Leaves will automatically be credited in your leave register in the starting of every quarter. Leaves will be credited at the rate of two leaves per quarter.  Leaves can be granted or refused subject to business emergencies.  A maximum of 10 days leaves can be taken at a stretch.  Every leave, must be approved, it begins with handing over the charge and ends on resume the charge.  No leave should prefix or suffix with weekends and any other defined

d ends on resume the charge.  No leave should prefix or suffix with weekends and any other defined holidays except emergencies. If so, the holidays and week offs will be counted as leave and will be deducted from leave balance or will be counted as loss of pay.  Leave taken without approval will attract loss of pay. More than 2 unapproved leaves will attract stop payment for all due payments.  Employees are expected in inform to their reporting manager in writing (on email) for all planned le

Employees are expected in inform to their reporting manager in writing (on email) for all planned leaves. A request mail must be sent 2 days in advance for 1 day leave, 5 days in advance for more than 1 leave and 1 month in advance for more than 3 days. 13 Confidential Document - HR ii. Casual leave-  Leaves are granted for certain unforeseen situations or where you are required to go for a day or two.  An employee is entitled for 8 days of casual leaves in a year (on a pro-rata basis).  An i

r two.  An employee is entitled for 8 days of casual leaves in a year (on a pro-rata basis).  An information regarding CL must be communicated to immediate reporter immediately and an update must be sent to him/her about the plan at earliest in case of extension. CLs must be approved on the day of resuming back to work.  For all Medical (sick) cases leaves for more than two days will require supported documents like prescription and reports to get it approved. The scanned copy of documents ca

pported documents like prescription and reports to get it approved. The scanned copy of documents can be given within five days. A fitness certificate needs to be submitted on the day of resuming back to work. iii. Leave without pay –  In case of exhaustion of leave balance, the further leave will attract Leave without Pay and also can be termed as Loss of Pay.  The other terms and conditions will remain the same. iv. Absent–  Leave would be counted as Absent in case of no information or bein

ll remain the same. iv. Absent–  Leave would be counted as Absent in case of no information or being rejected by immediate reporter. More than two absent leaves for more than two occurrences will attract a disciplinary action in case of no information before taking leave. v. Balance Carry forward and encashment-  At the end of every leave year, leave balances (PL only) of an employee will accrue, forwarded, or en-cashed based on the below rule: o The leave balance will be carried forward to ne

, forwarded, or en-cashed based on the below rule: o The leave balance will be carried forward to next year for a maximum of 4 leaves or balance leaves whichever is lower. The remaining balance (if any) will be proceed for encashment. vi. CL Balance-  At the end of every leave year, all un-availed CL is entitled for encashment.  Leave encashment will be calculated as follows: o (50% of gross monthly salary / 24 * Total CL balance) 14 Confidential Document - HR  3. Sexual Harassment in the Wor

thly salary / 24 * Total CL balance) 14 Confidential Document - HR  3. Sexual Harassment in the Workplace Policy Lucid outsourcing solutions is committed to maintaining a workplace free from sexual harassment. Sexual harassment is a form of workplace discrimination. All employees are required to work in a manner that prevents sexual harassment in the workplace. The company Policy is one component of the commitment to a discrimination-free work environment. The lucid policy applies to all employ

of the commitment to a discrimination-free work environment. The lucid policy applies to all employees, applicants for employment, internships, contractors, and persons conducting business, regardless of immigration status, with the company. The company will not tolerate sexual harassment. Any employee or individual covered by this policy who engages in sexual harassment or retaliation will be subject to remedial and disciplinary action (e.g., counseling, suspension, termination). Sexual harass

bject to remedial and disciplinary action (e.g., counseling, suspension, termination). Sexual harassment is offensive, is a violation of our policies, is unlawful, and may subject individually to liability for harm to targets of sexual harassment. Employees of every level who engage in sexual harassment, including managers and supervisors who engage in sexual harassment or knowingly allow such behavior to continue, will be penalized for such misconduct. The department will conduct a prompt and t

avior to continue, will be penalized for such misconduct. The department will conduct a prompt and thorough investigation that ensures due process for all the parties whenever management receives a complaint about sexual harassment or otherwise knows of possible sexual harassment occurring. Our Company will keep the investigation confidential to the extent allowed. Effective corrective action will take whenever sexual harassment is determined to have happened. All employees, including managers a

take whenever sexual harassment is determined to have happened. All employees, including managers and supervisors, should cooperate with any internal investigation of sexual harassment. What Is Sexual Harassment? Sexual harassment is a form of sex discrimination and is unlawful under federal, state, and (where applicable) local law. Sexual harassment includes harassment based on sex, sexual orientation, self-identified or perceived sex, gender expression, gender identity, and the status of bein

tation, self-identified or perceived sex, gender expression, gender identity, and the status of being transgender. Sexual harassment includes: Such conduct has the purpose or effect of unreasonably interfering with an individual's work performance or creating an intimidating, hostile, or offensive work environment, even if the reporting individual is not the intended target of the sexual harassment Such conduct is made either explicitly or implicitly a term or condition of employment. 15 Confide

t Such conduct is made either explicitly or implicitly a term or condition of employment. 15 Confidential Document - HR Submission to or rejection of such conduct is used as the basis for employment decisions affecting an individual's employment. Sexually harassing hostile work environment includes, but is not limited to, words, signs, jokes, pranks, intimidation, or physical violence which are sexual, or which are directed at an individual because of that individual's sex. Sexual harassment als

xual, or which are directed at an individual because of that individual's sex. Sexual harassment also occurs when a person in authority tries to trade job benefits for sexual favors. It can include hiring, promotion, continued employment, or any other terms, conditions, or privileges of employment. Employees who feel harassed should report it immediately so that the matter can be resolved. Any harassing conduct, even a single incident, can be addressed under this policy. Physical acts of a sexua

assing conduct, even a single incident, can be addressed under this policy. Physical acts of a sexual nature, such as: Touching, pinching, patting, kissing, hugging, grabbing, brushing against another employee's body, or poking another employee's body; Rape, sexual battery, molestation, or attempts to commit these assaults. Unwanted sexual advances or propositions, such as: Requests for sexual favors accompanied by implied or overt threats concerning the target's job performance evaluation, a pr

ors accompanied by implied or overt threats concerning the target's job performance evaluation, a promotion, or other job benefits or detriments; Subtle or obvious pressure for unwelcome sexual activities. Hostile actions will take against an individual because of that individual's sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, and the status of being transgender, such as: Interfering with, destroying, or damaging a person's workstation, tools, or equipment, or otherwise interfering with the individu

or damaging a person's workstation, tools, or equipment, or otherwise interfering with the individual's ability to perform the job; Sabotaging an individual's work; Bullying, yelling, name-calling.  4. Communications Policy Workplace policies need to be in place to establish boundaries and guidelines. It holds good for the IT and communication section too. 16 Confidential Document - HR The communication policies of an organization set expectations and manage the flow of communications within a

nication policies of an organization set expectations and manage the flow of communications within and outside the organization. They facilitate meaningful and necessary communication for employee productivity and morale without restricting communication. Having a good communication policy in place reduces conflicts and misunderstandings. Here are the various aspects: Employee Internal Communication Effective internal communication is a tool that is of utmost importance to maintaining transparen

on Effective internal communication is a tool that is of utmost importance to maintaining transparency within the company amongst employees. There are different modes of internal communications you can look forward to Lucid Outsourcing Solution Pvt. Ltd. Meetings Communications play a vital role in the meeting. Meetings are formal means of communication that flow continuously through all the departments. People handling functions that occur at multiple destinations and those who work independent

tments. People handling functions that occur at multiple destinations and those who work independently are required to meet regularly. In addition to this would be the Regular meetings of the department heads with Company management, to ensure smooth functioning.  5. Phone Usage Policy The purpose of this policy is to outline what the Company considers to be acceptable and unacceptable use of mobile phones at work. Mobile phones can be disruptive (in terms of productivity and concentration leve

ile phones at work. Mobile phones can be disruptive (in terms of productivity and concentration levels) and dangerous (from a health and safety point of view). Mobile phone use in the workplace can also risk breaches of confidentiality. This policy will apply to individual members of staff. What is acceptable will depend on the type of task and the working environment. We expect all employees to adopt a common-sense approach in this regard. Scope of policy All references to “mobile phones” in th

dopt a common-sense approach in this regard. Scope of policy All references to “mobile phones” in this policy include any personal mobile phone or electronic device capable of remote communication, such as a smartphone or a Personal Digital Assistant. This policy relates to all forms of communication, including, but not limited to, phone (and video) calls, text (or picture) messages, emails, WhatsApp, and instant messages. The “use” of a mobile phone also includes accessing the internet, for any

App, and instant messages. The “use” of a mobile phone also includes accessing the internet, for any purpose, on a mobile phone. Employees are 17 Confidential Document - HR not allowed to use their mobile phones to access social networking websites while they are working.  The use of cell phones is discouraged  While driving  While operating equipment  During office hours for gaming and entertainment  For indulging in long personal calls during office hours  During meetings  From recordin
